Ice_Log: 1700: A small iceberg in sight on weather bow, distant 4 miles, its length being about 100 feet, height above water 20 feet.
2030: Kept the ship jambed in the ~ to avoid collision with icebergs.

Note: 0400: I increaseded the 0400 and 0600 values for Dry and Water tempertures by 10: 44, 45 and 46 to 54, 55 and 56.
2400: I changed water temp to 75 from 55.
2000: I changed Dry value 87 to 78. The jump up and then down is not reasonable.

Other: 0330: At 3:30,  a very large red ball of fire fell from a small cloud, describing an arc of about 20o toward the Sd&Wd, and then disappeared; a few moments later another of about the same size, but of a blue color, shot from another cloud towards the Nd&Ed, each of the above while descending its course lighted up the ship and the water with its own peculiar color.

Note: 1400: The log has a dry temp of 90 which was later crossed out. I left it in.
